key_insight: Coplanar waveguide sensitivity can be optimized with a Beer--Lambert-style one-parameter rule, yielding a near-universal ~1 Np optimum that depends mainly on sample geometry and noise source.
engineering_relevance: This gives a practical design target for broadband magnetic resonance spectroscopy hardware, helping engineers choose CPW layouts that maximize sensitivity under real fabrication constraints.
method: Mapped CPW signal coupling and conductor loss onto the Beer--Lambert optimization problem.
Reduced sensitivity design to a universal one-parameter formulation independent of sample properties, frequency, and system losses.
Compared the framework against seven published broadband FMR instruments and fabrication limits.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports benchmarking against seven published broadband FMR instruments, showing PCB-milled designs are limited by minimum slot width while photolithographic designs approach the additive-noise optimum.
watch_item: The predicted optimum is constrained by fabrication limits, so the best geometry may be unreachable for a given manufacturing process or sample size.
paper_title: Sensitivity-optimal coplanar waveguide design for broadband magnetic resonance spectroscopy: a Beer--Lambert framework
